source.description: Six-panel raster process contact sheet generated for the June 30, 2026 backyard telescope lesson.
source.notes: The contact sheet must keep one telescope pose, crop, scale, and paper texture from construction through the finished sketch.

process_strategy: Each frame adds visible telescope structure before the final panel only darkens keeper lines, adds restrained graphite shading, and clarifies already placed grass and star marks.

final_elements.name: tilted telescope tube
final_elements.description: A long angled telescope tube pointing toward the upper right.

final_elements.name: front lens rim
final_elements.description: A circular rim at the sky-facing end of the telescope.

final_elements.name: eyepiece
final_elements.description: A small eyepiece at the lower end of the tube.

final_elements.name: finder scope
final_elements.description: A small tube sitting on top of the main telescope.

final_elements.name: tripod mount and legs
final_elements.description: A central mount with three simple tripod legs supporting the telescope.

final_elements.name: backyard grass line
final_elements.description: A few light ground strokes under the tripod feet.

final_elements.name: small stars
final_elements.description: A few simple star marks around the telescope's pointing direction.

final_elements.name: restrained graphite shading
final_elements.description: Soft shading on the tube, lens rim, mount, and ground.

frames.visible_job: Places the tilted tube guide, center line, and light mount guide.
Adds the front lens rim and small eyepiece while keeping the same tube angle.
Refines the tube contour and adds the small finder scope on top.
Adds the mount, three tripod legs, and a few ground strokes.
Adds small star marks and light shading to existing telescope parts.

final_step.visible_job: Darkens keeper contours, clarifies the lens, strengthens the tripod, and deepens the already placed shading and stars.

rejection_checks: No telescope part, tripod leg, star, or ground shape should first appear in the final image.
No frame may shade or darken a telescope part that was not already visible.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same telescope angle, scale, and placement as the step frames.
